page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors. It features 180 vCPUs mapped to 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled, delivering a dedicated x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is equipped with 1440 GB of RAM, establishing an 8 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 277249.17 and a PassMark CPU score of 124350.65. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 13485.99 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 349.44 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The platform also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in Redis SET operations, handling 210.86 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 84.78 GB/sec.

The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate cryptographic and vector calculations. This is reflected in top-tier performance for software compilation, ray tracing, and LLM inference prompt processing. However, single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and single-core Geekbench execution, fall into the average to strong categories, indicating that workloads must be highly parallelized to fully leverage the hardware.

Given its high resource density and top-tier multi-core and memory benchmarks, the c3d-highmem-180 offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, software compilation pipelines, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server configured with 180 vCPUs and 1440 GB of RAM. Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors on an x86_64 architecture, this instance features 90 physical cores with dedicated allocation and no bundled local storage or GPUs. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 277249.17 and uncached read memory bandwidth of 349.44 GB/sec. The processor supports AVX-512 instruction sets, facilitating rapid cryptographic and vector operations. While single-core performance is strong, the instance's primary strength lies in its massive parallel processing capabilities and high memory density. It is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, Redis deployments, large-scale software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized instance with 180 vCPUs and 1440 GB RAM, featuring AMD EPYC processors and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 features 180 vCPUs, 1440 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 180 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors.
1440 GB of RAM providing an 8 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing benchmarks.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis, and CPU-based LLM inference.
